coronary-artery-surgery.title: Coronary Artery Surgery
coronary-artery-surgery.shortDescription: Bypass procedures that build a new path around blocked coronary vessels.
coronary-artery-surgery.description: When coronary arteries narrow or block, bypass surgery restores circulation to the heart muscle. Wherever possible, our approach favours minimally invasive techniques.

midcab.title: MIDCAB
midcab.shortDescription: Minimally invasive direct coronary bypass — through a small incision below the left breast.
midcab.description: A 5–8 cm incision in the left chest wall allows bypass without dividing the breastbone. Faster recovery, less pain, and a smaller scar.

off-pump-bypass.title: Off-pump bypass (beating-heart)
off-pump-bypass.shortDescription: Coronary bypass performed without the heart-lung machine, on a beating heart.
off-pump-bypass.description: Avoiding the cardiopulmonary bypass pump can reduce kidney and neurological complications in selected patients.

classic-cabg.title: Classic open bypass
classic-cabg.description: The standard, evidence-based approach for multi-vessel disease and complex anatomy. Excellent long-term graft patency.

heart-valve-surgery.title: Heart Valve Surgery
heart-valve-surgery.shortDescription: Repair or replacement options for aortic, mitral, and tricuspid valve disease.
heart-valve-surgery.description: Valve narrowing or leakage strains the heart and causes breathlessness. Solutions are tailored by age, valve type, and risk profile — including minimally invasive and transcatheter options.

tavi.title: TAVI
tavi.shortDescription: Transcatheter aortic valve implantation — for patients at high surgical risk.
tavi.description: Typically for older or higher-risk patients. A new valve is delivered via the femoral artery, without opening the chest. Hospital stays are short.

sutureless-aortic.title: Sutureless aortic valve replacement
sutureless-aortic.shortDescription: A biological aortic valve that anchors without sutures.
sutureless-aortic.description: Shortens operative time and reduces aortic cross-clamp time — an alternative for higher-risk surgical candidates.

mitral-repair.title: Mitral valve repair
mitral-repair.shortDescription: Repair that preserves the patient’s own valve whenever feasible.
mitral-repair.description: Where appropriate, performed minimally invasively via right mini-thoracotomy. Long-term outcomes are superior to replacement.

mitral-replacement.title: Mitral valve replacement
mitral-replacement.shortDescription: When repair is not possible, replacement with a mechanical or biological prosthesis.
mitral-replacement.description: The valve type is selected based on the patient’s age and suitability for long-term anticoagulation.

tricuspid.title: Tricuspid valve surgery
tricuspid.shortDescription: Performed alongside left-heart surgery or for isolated tricuspid disease.
tricuspid.description: Most often combined with mitral surgery. Minimally invasive options exist for isolated tricuspid regurgitation.

aortic-surgery.title: Aortic Surgery
aortic-surgery.shortDescription: Surgical treatment for aortic aneurysms, dissections, and root disease.
aortic-surgery.description: The aorta is the body’s main artery — dilation or tearing is life-threatening. We cover the full spectrum of emergency and elective aortic surgery, including valve- and root-preserving procedures.

ascending-aneurysm.title: Ascending aortic aneurysm
ascending-aneurysm.shortDescription: Dilation of the first segment of the thoracic aorta.
ascending-aneurysm.description: Above a certain diameter, the risk of rupture rises sharply. Replacement with a prosthetic graft is standard; the valve and root may be addressed together when needed.

aortic-dissection.title: Aortic dissection
aortic-dissection.shortDescription: An emergency caused by a tear in the aortic wall.
aortic-dissection.description: Type A dissection is life-threatening and demands repair within hours. 24/7 emergency capability is a core priority of our team.

bentall.title: Bentall procedure
bentall.shortDescription: Combined replacement of the aortic valve, root, and ascending aorta.
bentall.description: The standard procedure when valve disease accompanies root dilation. A composite valved graft is implanted.

david.title: David procedure
david.description: When the valve itself is intact, only the root is replaced — preserving the patient’s native valve for a long-lasting result.

other-cardiac-surgery.title: Other Adult Cardiac Surgery
other-cardiac-surgery.shortDescription: Cardiac tumours, pericardial disease, and surgery for atrial fibrillation.
other-cardiac-surgery.description: Specialist surgical solutions for cardiac conditions outside the standard bypass and valve domains, but no less impactful on a patient’s life.

atrial-myxoma.title: Atrial myxoma excision
atrial-myxoma.shortDescription: Removal of the most common benign cardiac tumour.
atrial-myxoma.description: Usually located in the left atrium and capable of causing embolism or obstruction. Surgical outcomes are excellent.

pericardiectomy.title: Pericardiectomy
pericardiectomy.shortDescription: Removal of the pericardium for conditions such as constrictive pericarditis.
pericardiectomy.description: When the pericardium thickens, cardiac filling is restricted; pericardiectomy markedly relieves breathlessness and oedema.

maze.title: MAZE procedure
maze.description: Surgically interrupts the electrical pathways that cause fibrillation, restoring sinus rhythm. May be combined with other cardiac surgery.
